zh.Horsley, Belper, Derbyshire, England: 英國英格蘭德比夏爾霍斯利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Eyton於大約1300年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾時，她的父親John de Eyton27歲，母親Isabella Curzon21歲。她於1320年之前與Sir Henry de Cresswelle結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世於1383年在英國諾圖波藍德武德享年84歲。

zh-hans.Horsley, Belper, Derbyshire, England: 英国英格兰德比夏爾霍斯利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Eyton于大约1300年出生在英国英格兰史洛普爾时，她的父亲John de Eyton27岁，母亲Isabella Curzon21岁。她于1320年之前与Sir Henry de Cresswelle结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。她去世于1383年在英国諾圖波藍德武德享年84岁。

nameMeanings.name: Eyton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Eyton (Shrops) or Eaton (Cheshire, Leics, Staffs), or Eaton Bray (Beds), Cold Eaton or Long Eaton (Derbys).

variants.name: Ayton
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from Ayton (North Yorkshire) or Ayton (Berwickshire). The former is derived from Old Norse á ‘river, stream’ + tūn ‘farmstead’. The later takes its name from the River Eye (likely from Old English ēa ‘river, stream’) + tūn ‘settlement’.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Eyton was born about 1300, in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, John de Eyton, was 27 and her mother, Isabella Curzon, was 21. She married Sir Henry de Cresswelle before 1320. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died in 1383, in Woodhorn, Northumberland, England, at the age of 84.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan d’Eyton est née environ 1300, à Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John d’Eyton, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Isabella Curzon, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Henry de Cresswelle avant 1320. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1383, à Woodhorn, Northumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 84 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Eyton um 1300 in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de Eyton 27 und ihre Mutter Isabella Curzon 21. Sie hat vor 1320 Sir Henry de Cresswelle geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1383 im Alter von 84 Jahren in Woodhorn, Northumberland, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Eyton è nata il circa 1300 nel Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, John de Eyton, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Isabella Curzon, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Sir Henry de Cresswelle prima del 1320. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì nel 1383, nel Woodhorn, Northumberland, England, all'età di 84 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Eytonが約1300年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアで生まれた時，父John de Eytonは27歳，母Isabella Curzonは21歳であった。1320年以前にSir Henry de Cresswelleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1383年にイギリスノースウンバーランドウッドホールンで84歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Eyton(이)가 대략 1300년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Eyton(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabella Curzon(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1320년 이전 Sir Henry de Cresswelle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1383년 영국 노르스벌랜드 우드호른에서 84세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Eyton nasceu aproximadamente 1300, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John de Eyton, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Isabella Curzon, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Henry de Cresswelle antes 1320. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1383, em Woodhorn, Northumberland, Inglaterra, com 84 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Eyton родилась около 1300 г. в местности шропшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John de Eyton было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabella Curzon, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Henry de Cresswelle перед 1320 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1383 г. в местности вудхаурн, нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 84 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Eyton nació aproximadamente 1300, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John de Eyton, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Isabella Curzon, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Sir Henry de Cresswelle antes de 1320. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió en 1383, en Woodhorn, Northumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 84 años.
